state_history.note: Market probability moved after updated weather data.

penalties.note: Spread may erase expected value unless entered patiently.
Resolution rule and data-source timing require manual review.

score.scoring_notes: Interesting model-vs-market gap, but liquidity and rules are gating issues.

hypothesis: The event market may be slow to incorporate updated weather-model data, creating a temporary probability gap.
evidence: Kalshi YES probability moved materially during the last scan window.
External weather-model update appears directionally supportive.
The market has not fully repriced to the internal probability estimate.
counter_evidence: Bid/ask spread is wide enough to damage expected value.
Resolution rule risk may be larger than the simple model gap suggests.
confirmation_needed: Verify resolution source and exact settlement language.
Check orderbook depth and spread immediately before any paper entry.
Confirm the external weather update is still current.
invalidation: Market reprices to internal probability before entry.
Updated weather run reverses the signal.
Resolution rules make the edge ambiguous.
decision_reason: Needs memo-level rule review before it can become a paper candidate.

model_vs_market.market_belief: Market price implies the event is close to a coin flip.
model_vs_market.internal_view: Weather-model data suggests probability may be higher than market price.
model_vs_market.notes: Example only; no live market claim.

notes: Example candidate for prediction-market model-vs-market workflow.
